title: Money = Time + Value
learningObjectives: Understand that money represents exchanged time and effort
Recognize the Spender vs Owner mindset
See how small daily choices compound over time
keyPrinciples: Every dollar you earn cost you time to create
Owners think about money working FOR them
Spenders trade money for short-term feelings
storyPrompts: {name} just got their first paycheck — $80 for a weekend of yard work. Walking past the mall, a glowing display catches their eye: the newest limited-edition sneakers, marked down to exactly $80. 'I earned this,' {name} thinks. 'But so did every dollar I've ever spent.' The question isn't whether they CAN buy it — it's whether they SHOULD.

After three months of dog-sitting, {name} has saved up {amount}. Their friend just blew their savings on a gaming chair they barely use. 'That's their money,' {name} shrugs — but they can't help wondering what the smarter play looks like.

{name}'s older cousin owns a small vending machine route. 'I don't trade hours for dollars anymore,' he says. 'My machines earn while I sleep.' {name} stares at their crumpled {amount} and wonders: what would an Owner do right now?

decisionCards.scenario: {name} has $80 in pocket — the exact cost of those hyped sneakers.
decisionCards.context: It took 8 hours of work to earn this. The sneakers will be 'old news' in 3 months.

choices.label: Buy the sneakers
choices.description: You earned it, live a little. Instant flex.
choices.opportunityCost: That $60 invested at 10%/yr would be $160 in 10 years.

choices.label: Save 80% + spend 20%
choices.description: Put $64 in savings, treat yourself to $16 of something small.
choices.opportunityCost: You still enjoyed the moment AND built your future.

choices.label: Invest the whole amount
choices.description: All $80 goes into your invest bucket. Pure owner move.
choices.opportunityCost: No treat today — but compound interest starts NOW.

decisionCards.scenario: A friend offers to sell you their old game console for {amount}.
decisionCards.context: It's a good deal — but you already have a console that works fine.

choices.label: Buy it, great deal!
choices.description: It IS cheaper than retail...
choices.opportunityCost: A deal is only good if you needed it in the first place.

choices.label: Pass, I'm good
choices.description: You recognize you don't actually need it.

choices.label: Negotiate and flip it
choices.description: Buy it low, sell it higher to someone who DOES need it.

decisionCards.scenario: You get a birthday cash gift of $50. Your options are clear.

choices.label: Blow it on fun stuff
choices.description: Celebrate! It's birthday money!

choices.label: Split: $20 spend, $30 save
choices.description: Enjoy some AND keep some.

choices.label: Triple-bucket split
choices.description: $15 spend, $15 save, $20 invest.

decisionCards.scenario: You see an ad: 'Earn $500 FAST with this one trick!'
decisionCards.context: Your Spender friend already clicked. What do you do?

choices.label: Click it! Get rich quick!
choices.description: It sounds legit enough...
choices.opportunityCost: Scam risk is real. Your $20 is gone.

choices.label: Research it first
choices.description: Spend 10 minutes verifying before spending a dime.

choices.label: Ignore it entirely
choices.description: Real wealth is built slowly, not in one click.

decisionCards.scenario: Lunch line: the $3 item vs the {amount} meal that 'looks better'.
decisionCards.context: Small decisions compound. This happens 5 days a week.

choices.label: Upgrade every time
choices.description: Life is short, eat well.

choices.label: Pack lunch from home
choices.description: Save that restaurant money.

choices.label: Budget day: basic + a treat on Fridays
choices.description: Discipline with planned fun.

decisionCards.scenario: Your older sibling says: 'Money doesn't buy happiness.' You respond...
decisionCards.context: Deep thought moment. What do YOU actually believe?

choices.label: 'You're right, I don't need to save.'
choices.description: Use it as an excuse to spend more.

choices.label: 'True — but security creates freedom.'
choices.description: Recognize money = options, not just things.

choices.label: 'So I'll make enough to choose.'
choices.description: Ownership mindset: build until you have options.

decisionCards.scenario: Your classmate brags about their new {item}. You feel the urge...
decisionCards.context: Keeping up with others is the fastest way to go broke.

choices.label: Buy the same thing ASAP
choices.description: Can't be out-flexed.

choices.label: Feel it, then let it go
choices.description: Notice the urge without acting on it.

choices.label: Ask: 'How'd you pay for it?'
choices.description: Get curious about their actual financial situation.

decisionCards.scenario: End of month: $30 left over after all expenses. What now?
decisionCards.context: The Spender sees spending money. The Owner sees seed money.

choices.label: Spend it — you 'deserve' it
choices.description: Reward yourself for surviving the month.

choices.label: Save half, invest half
choices.description: $15 each. The Owner move.

choices.label: Invest it all
choices.description: You lived fine without it — let it work.

bossChallengeTemplates.scenario: BOSS: The mall is having a 'one day only' sale. Everything you want is 40% off.
bossChallengeTemplates.context: Sales are designed to trigger urgency. This is the boss testing your resolve.

choices.label: Go shopping — it's a great deal!
choices.description: 40% off is real money saved...right?

choices.label: Only buy what was already on your list
choices.description: Pre-planned purchases only. The Owner's rule.

choices.label: Skip the sale entirely
choices.description: The best deal is not spending at all.

bossChallengeTemplates.scenario: BOSS: Your friend group is going to a concert — tickets are {amount} each.
bossChallengeTemplates.context: FOMO is one of the oldest spending triggers in the book.

choices.label: Buy the ticket, memories matter
choices.description: You only live once!

choices.label: Suggest a free alternative
choices.description: Propose hanging out somewhere free instead.

choices.label: Budget for it by cutting elsewhere
choices.description: You want to go — so find the money elsewhere first.

bossChallengeTemplates.scenario: BOSS: Your piggy bank is full. You can hear the coins rattling. What do you DO with it?
bossChallengeTemplates.context: Cash sitting still isn't working for you.

choices.label: Leave it — it feels safe
choices.description: At least it's still there.

choices.label: Move it to a savings account
choices.description: Earns a little interest. Better than a jar.

choices.label: Split: savings + first investment
choices.description: Half saved, half put to work in Ownership Orchard.

forecasts: You made disciplined choices this week — temptation ads will appear less often next week. Keep it up!
You broke your saving streak. Expect stronger impulse temptations next week. Stay sharp!
Your Ownership trait is climbing. New investment opportunities appear next week!
High temptation meter detected. Next week will test your patience even harder.
